# Source of Truth

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)**

## The Commitment

Every domain of information has exactly ONE authoritative source. When multiple
sources exist, one is designated authoritative and the others are explicitly
derived. When sources conflict, the authoritative source wins. No domain
has dual or ambiguous authority.

## What This Means

- Every data domain declares its source of truth.
- Derived sources (caches, summaries, copies) are labeled as such.
- Conflict resolution is deterministic: authoritative source wins, conflict is logged.
- Creating a new information source requires declaring its relationship to existing sources.

## Violation Looks Like

Project status tracked in three places (spreadsheet, database, and meeting notes)
with no declared authority. Two systems holding contradictory versions of the
same data with no mechanism to determine which is correct.

## Derived From

- JOS-C05 (Data Primacy) — data integrity requires single authority
- JOS-C07 (Knowledge Decay) — multiple sources accelerate decay through drift
